title: Web-Scale Datasets
concept: Modern LLMs require trillions of tokens for training, sourced from public web dumps, books, code repositories, and scientific articles.

exercises.prompt: How much data do modern Large Language Models (LLMs) need for training?

options.text: Trillions of tokens from the web, books, and code

exercises.explain: Large Language Models require massive amounts of text—often trillions of tokens—to learn the complexities of human language.

cards.title: Feeding a Giant Brain
cards.body: How does an AI learn to write poetry, debug computer code, and explain quantum physics all at once? It doesn't study just one textbook—it reads a significant portion of the entire public internet! Let's explore the massive datasets that make AI smart.

cards.title: Web-Scale Datasets
cards.body: A Web-Scale Dataset is an enormous collection of text containing trillions of words (tokens). It is gathered from across the public internet, including websites, digitized books, open-source code repositories, and academic papers, to train Large Language Models.

cards.title: The Ultimate Library
cards.body: Imagine you are preparing to write the ultimate encyclopedia. You can't just read one school textbook. Instead, you build a massive library by collecting every public book, newspaper, website, and scientific paper you can find. Training an LLM is just like building and reading that massive library before starting to write.

cards.title: What's in the Mix?
cards.body: To build a powerful AI helper, engineers use datasets like Common Crawl (which copies billions of public web pages), collections of millions of books, and repositories of public computer code. This diverse mix ensures the AI learns everything from casual chatting to complex programming.

cards.title: Not Just Encyclopedias
cards.body: People often think AI models are trained on just a few specialized encyclopedias to stay smart. In reality, they need trillions of tokens from messy, diverse, public web dumps and code platforms to understand how humans actually write and communicate.

cards.title: Data Scale Comparison
cards.body: Compare how AI training data has evolved from early models to modern web-scale models.

right.text: Trained on trillions of tokens from web crawls, libraries of books, code, and scientific papers.

left.text: Trained on small, clean datasets like a single encyclopedia or a few thousand news articles.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes the sources used to build web-scale datasets for LLMs?

options.text: Only social media posts from the last week.
Public web pages, books, code repositories, and scientific articles.
Private text messages and personal emails.

exercises.explain: Web-scale datasets are built by collecting diverse public sources, including websites, books, scientific articles, and programming code.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Modern LLMs can reach human-level general knowledge by training on just a few thousand document files.
exercises.explain: LLMs require trillions of tokens from diverse sources like web dumps, books, and code to learn general language skills. A small set of documents is not enough.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the fact about AI training size.
exercises.sentence: Modern Large Language Models require training on datasets containing ________ of tokens.
exercises.explain: To capture all the nuances of human language and knowledge, AI models must analyze trillions of words (tokens) during their training phase.

exercises.prompt: Identify which statement is spoken by an AI describing its own background.

options.text: I found this recipe by looking through my grandmother's handwritten cookbook in the kitchen.
I searched for the flight status using a popular travel search engine on my phone.
I generated this response after analyzing patterns in my web-scale dataset of trillions of tokens.

exercises.explain: The third option is generated by an AI because it mentions having access to a "web-scale dataset of trillions of tokens," which is the terminology for AI training data, whereas the others refer to standard human libraries and search engines.

exercises.prompt: How do modern Large Language Models (LLMs) get enough information to understand and generate human-like text?

options.text: They are trained on a few thousand high-quality textbooks hand-written by AI engineers.
They only read private emails and personal text messages.
They learn from trillions of tokens scraped from public websites, books, code, and papers.
They generate their own data from scratch without reading any human-made text.

exercises.explain: Modern LLMs require trillions of words (tokens) to learn grammar, facts, and reasoning. This massive volume of data can only be gathered by combining diverse, web-scale sources like public websites, books, programming code, and academic articles.
